missions.title: Introduce yourself
missions.scenario: You meet a new colleague for the first time.
missions.promptContext: The learner is completing an A1 calibration. Practice a brief first meeting with a new colleague. Ask for a name, origin, and one simple personal detail. Use short French lines and English support only when the learner needs it. Do not claim a CEFR result or mastery.

steps.evidenceGoal: Recognise basic first-person French.
Recognise basic first-person French.
Recognise basic first-person French.
Recognise basic first-person French.
Recognise basic first-person French.
Recognise basic first-person French.
Understand personal details in a short introduction.
Give a supported first introduction aloud.

missions.title: Order at a café
missions.scenario: You order politely and respond to a price at a café.
missions.promptContext: The learner is practising a polite café order. Marie plays the server. Keep the exchange realistic, short, and tied to ordering, price, and thanks. Let the learner speak before offering a correction.

steps.evidenceGoal: Understand an order and its price.
Make a polite request aloud.

missions.title: Describe your day
missions.scenario: You tell a new colleague about your work and daily routine.
missions.promptContext: The learner is practising an A2 workday description. Marie is a new colleague. Keep the grammar in the present tense and encourage one short answer at a time.

steps.evidenceGoal: Understand a colleague describing their workday.
Describe your daily routine aloud in the present tense.

missions.title: Explain your work experience
missions.scenario: You answer a question about your professional experience.
missions.promptContext: The learner is practising a B1 professional introduction. Marie plays an interviewer. Ask one short follow-up at a time. The learner should connect present work, previous experience, and motivation. Coach briefly only when needed.

steps.evidenceGoal: Identify experience and motivation in a professional answer.
Connect work experience in a short response.

missions.title: Defend an opinion
missions.scenario: You give a balanced opinion and respond to a challenge.
missions.promptContext: The learner is practising a B2 opinion task. Marie asks for a clear position, one example, and a response to one counterargument. Keep the conversation natural and do not award a score or claim exam readiness.

steps.evidenceGoal: Structure a clear written position with support.
Give and support an opinion in conversation.
